label: Playbook mismatch — RFQ matter, NDA prompt

events.text: Quick aside on this matter — can you draft a generic NDA template that we'd use with any prospective bidder before sharing pricing or volume data? Keep it short, mutual, 2-year term, English law.

playbook.rationale: Negative-guard discrimination test. RFQ playbook IS in scope (matter is RFQ-flavoured) but the task is NDA drafting — RFQ playbook noun doesn't match NDA task noun. NDA playbook is NOT in scope (only RFQ playbook installed). Agent should read NEITHER playbook.

skill.rationale: nda-review is a review skill (procedure for reviewing an incoming NDA), not a drafting skill. The ask is to author a template from scratch. Agent should produce drafted prose, not load_skill.

delegate.rationale: Single item draft. No quantity word.

redline.rationale: Drafting from scratch, not redlining an existing document.

notes: Re-uses Sprint 30 RFQ fixture corpus (Pemberton docs in matter folder) but the prompt is unrelated to the in-folder documents. The RFQ playbook is installed but the topic mismatch means the agent should NOT open it. This is the cleanest negative-guard discrimination test in Sprint 32 — both playbook negative guards (wrong-noun) and skill negative guards (wrong-verb-shape) fire here. Sprint 32 expansion.
